title: CeMbio Screen 96WP
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ulvg","text":"Protocol for behavioural screening of Caenorhabditis elegans response to bacteria from its natural microbiome (CeMbio)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":38,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using an eyebrow hairpick, pick 10 L4-stage N2 worms onto each of 10 OP50-seeded 90mm petri plates 4 days prior to bleaching (e.g. on Monday if bleaching on Friday).
On day of bleaching (e.g. Friday 2pm) follow the protocol for Bleach synchronisation of C. elegans: Bleach synchronisation of C. elegans by Ida Barlow

Re-feed arrested L1 larvae approximately 72 hours prior to tracking (e.g. on Monday if tracking on Thursday), following the steps outlined in the above protocol for Bleach synchronisation of C. elegans.
Store the refed L1 plates in 20°C incubator.

02:30:00 2 days prior to tracking (e.g. on Tuesday if tracking on Thursday), make 500ml LB NGM agar following the protocol Making LB NGM Plates: Making LB NGM Plates by Priota Islam 1.5 g, Sodium Chloride (Sigma-Aldrich-71376-1KG) 8.5 g, Bio Agar (Biogene-400-050) 1.25 g, LB Broth Powder (Fisher. BioReagents-BP-1426-2) 0.5 mL, Cholesterol, 5mg/ml in EtOH (Sigma-C1145-250MG) 487.5 mL, Sterile Water Post-autoclave salts: 0.5 mL, Calcium Chloride, 1M (CaCl2; Sigma-C3881-1KG) 0.5 mL, Magnesium Sulphate, 1M (MgSO4; Fisher-M/1050/53) 12.5 mL, Potassium Sulphate, 1M, (KPO4, pH 6.0; Sigma-Aldrich-P0662-500G-M)
Using the Integra ViaFill, dispense 200ul of LB NGM agar into 96-well imaging plates, following the protocol Dispensing agar into multiwell plates: Dispensing agar into multiwell plates by Saul Moore
Leave the plates on the lab bench (with lids on) until the agar is dry.

3 days prior to tracking (e.g. on Tuesday if tracking Thursday), inoculate overnight cultures of bacterial strains in 96-well from 96-well CeMbio frozen stock plates, following the protocol for Growing overnight bacterial culture in 96WP: Growing overnight bacterial culture in 96WP by Priota Islam Place inoculated cultures in 96WP in a shaking incubator at 25°C, 200rpm.

The next day (e.g. Wednesday, 1 day prior to tracking), remove overnight cultures from the shaking incubators and replace the LB in each of the wells of the inoculated 96WPs (that are reserved for the control bacterial strain) with 200ul OP50 bacterial culture that was inoculated separately.
On the day of tracking (e.g. Thursday, if re-feeding was performed on Monday), remove seeded plates from 4°C, and dry for 30 mins under a hood.
00:15:00 After acclimation under the Hydra rigs, record worm behaviour on the bacterial food for 15 minutes (25fps, exposure 25000msec, bluelight stimulation).
Record 15 minute videos at the following timepoints: 1, 3, 5, and 24 hours on food.
After tracking, discard the plates in a biological waste bin.
Separately inoculate 100ml E. coli OP50 bacteria in an Erlenmeyer flask and place in a shaking incubator at 37°C, 200rpm.
Remove imaging plates from 4°C storage and record the average weight of 3 of the plates.
Place seeded plates to dry under a hood for about 30 minutes.
Store the bacterial cultures at 4°C until seeding bacterial lawns later that day.
Once imaging plates are dry, remove bacterial cultures from 4°C and seed 10ul bacterial culture into 96-well imaging plates, following the protocol Seeding bacterial lawns using Integra ViaFlo (96WP)
Measure the weight of 3 plates (with lids on) and record average plate weight on day of pouring.
Store the plates upside-down at 4°C (plates can be stored for up to 1 week prior to use).
Place the imaging plates in a drying cabinet for approximately 2-3 hours (until the plates have lost approximately 3-5% of their original weight).
Dispense Day1 worms onto seeded plates for imaging, following the protocol Using the worm sorter (COPAS): COPAS wormsorter by Ida Barlow
04:00:00 Leave seeded plates on the lab bench (with lids on) for 4 hours to allow lawns to grow.
72:00:00 Keep the tube with bleached worms on a rotator in an incubator at 20°C until re-feeding (maximum 5 days post-bleaching, as behaviour is not consistent after this time frame).
Remove worms on 90mm Petri plates from 20°C incubator, and follow the protocol Preparing worms for the COPAS (wormsorter): Preparing worms for the COPAS (wormsorter) by Priota Islam
Store seeded plates at 4°C overnight.
00:30:00 Leave plates to dry under a hood for 30 minutes.
00:30:00 Place plates in the imaging cave under the Hydra rigs to acclimate for 30 minutes prior to imaging (+1hr timepoint).
creator: Saul Moore
